goal: Review the STM32 portion of the audit document and remediate the firmware issues that are still valid and can be solved in software within this repository.
deliverable: A triaged mapping from audit findings to current STM32 firmware state
Concrete firmware changes for the approved remediation scope
A diagnosis-backed remediation for USART3/CP2102 freeze risk and jerky host-driven motion
Build-oriented verification evidence and cleanup receipts
constraints: Focus only on the lower machine / STM32 firmware side.
Prioritize software-only changes in this repository; hardware redesign items can be documented but not implemented.
Do not regress current serial protocol behavior without explicit requirement changes.
acceptance_criteria: Each implemented fix traces back to a still-valid STM32 audit finding.
The USART3 receive/transmit path is made less likely to stall the whole firmware under host traffic.
Host-driven wheel motion becomes smoother by removing obvious low-speed pulse-inducing control behavior.
Changes build successfully or a concrete blocker is documented with evidence.
Final response distinguishes fixed items, deferred hardware-only items, and residual risks.
non_goals: Implementing every audit suggestion if it is already fixed or out of scope for firmware-only work
assumptions: The user wants direct remediation, not only a report.
The highest-value firmware items are the issues still present in current code, not the full historical audit list.
Plan approval is required before code edits because the vibe runtime is in interactive governed mode.

already_addressed_or_stale: S3-001 watchdog feed only once: current main loop feeds watchdog continuously
likely_still_valid: S3-002 encoder fault does not escalate to active stop/fault handling
S3-003 control cycle is still tick-polling instead of timer-driven
S3-007 UART ISR still performs parser work
S3-010 command-timeout behavior is basic and should be re-checked against safety expectations
S3-016 IMU failure handling should be reviewed for full disable/degrade behavior
USART3 host link can stall system responsiveness under blocking transmit plus IRQ-heavy receive behavior
Closed-loop low-speed behavior is likely jerky because Ki is zero while startup assist injects short duty pulses

new_user_observations.freeze: USART3 connected through on-board CP2102 to host can cause the whole system to appear dead; pressing reset is ineffective and power-cycle is required.
new_user_observations.jerky_motion: When the upper machine commands motion, wheels rotate intermittently rather than smoothly.
